evidence_quotes.quote: protecting video games that have been sold to people from being taken down with no alternative method to access the game the consumer has purchased.
Much like it has already put in place protection from pushing gambling and other predatory systems into video games.

paragraphs.text: Hello! I would kindly like to request looking into including video game protection in the Digital Fairness Act. Much like it has already put in place protection from pushing gambling and other predatory systems into video games. I think it should also include protecting video games that have been sold to people from being taken down with no alternative method to access the game the consumer has purchased. A lot of modern video games rely on servers provided by the game's publishers. However if we could for example provide the community tools to set up their own servers AFTER the publishers want to shut a game down it would be a huge step in protecting consumers from purchasing games that become unusable. I'm not able to relay the full scope of what all of this could mean but I think the Stop Killing Games movement has a solid overview of ways that games are made unusable and how to provide solutions that are fair to both the game developers/publishers and the consumer. Thank you for reading and I hope to hear more about your view on this and if it is indeed something that could be included in the Digital Fairness Act.
